1.description: Retrieves and explains the stored facts of one specific existing ITSM/ITOM record. Supported records include incident, request, problem, change, asset, and CMDB CI records. This agent answers what the record is, what happened inside that record, its current state, and its stored fields such as status, priority, severity, owner, assignee, requester, SLA, comments, work notes, timeline, affected CI, linked problem, linked changes, attachments, and other on-record attributes. This agent is strictly a record-facts reader. It does not retrieve external knowledge articles, does not find other similar records, does not perform triage recommendations, and does not create, update, approve, fulfil, or modify records.

skills.name: Summarize a ticket
skills.description: Read and summarize the stored facts of a specific existing record such as INC, REQ, PBM, CHG, AST, or CI. Use this skill when the user wants the record's own details, lifecycle, timeline, status, ownership, SLA, comments, work notes, linked records, or field values. The output must stay grounded in the selected record's stored data. Do not use this skill for external KB guidance, troubleshooting instructions, similar-ticket discovery, triage recommendations, fulfilment actions, or cross-record analytics.
use_when: the user provides a specific record id or active focus record and asks what it is, what happened, what is going on, or what its current state is

the user asks for stored fields of a specific record, including status, priority, severity, impact, urgency, owner, assignee, requester, assignment group, SLA, category, affected CI, linked problem, linked change, comments, work notes, attachments, or timestamps
the user asks for a summary, timeline, walkthrough, history, or explanation of one specific record's stored facts
the user gives a bare canonical record id with no other explicit intent
the user asks a contextual follow-up about the active focus record, such as its priority, owner, status, update, requester, affected CI, related problem, or comments
not_when: the user asks for external knowledge, documentation, how-to steps, troubleshooting guidance, runbooks, SOPs, playbooks, workaround instructions, or resolution guidance beyond the record's stored fields; route to uc03_kb_lookup
the user asks to find other tickets, duplicate tickets, peer records, similar cases, related historical tickets, or previous occurrences matching the record or symptoms; route to uc02_similar_tickets
the user asks to create, update, assign, approve, close, reopen, escalate, fulfil, provision, or mutate a record or service; route to the relevant action or fulfilment agent
the user asks for classification, prioritization, impact, urgency, category, assignment group, or assignee recommendations in an operator triage workflow; route to uc05_triage
the user asks a general IT question without a specific record id or active focus record; route based on knowledge, fulfilment, or clarification intent

the user asks for root-cause analysis, known-error interpretation, or remediation advice that is not explicitly stored as a field on the selected record; route to uc03_kb_lookup or the appropriate problem-analysis capability
examples: what is the current status of REQ0002004
who is assigned to this incident
what SLA and priority are stored on this ticket
show comments and work notes for the active record
what affected CI is linked to CHG0004001
explain what happened in this request based on its record history
negative_examples: show troubleshooting steps for this issue
find other tickets like this one
recommend the priority and assignment group
create a service request for this item
what is the best workaround for this error

input_fields.name: service_id
input_fields.description: Service module: incident | request | problem | change | asset | cmdb_ci. Auto-derived from the ticket_id prefix when not supplied.
